description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese sake barrel, known as a 'komodaru', wrapped in woven straw with thick braided rope securing the top and sides. The exterior features hand-painted Japanese characters and a prominent illustration of a beckoning cat (maneki-neko), a symbol of good fortune, on a white background. The barrel is cylindrical, with a slightly bulging middle and a flat top and bottom, and the straw wrapping gives it a rustic, textured appearance. One side displays a red and white label with additional Japanese text, while another side shows more detailed kanji inscriptions. The ropes are tightly knotted, with some ends left loose, adding to the artisanal look. The overall appearance suggests it is a decorative or ceremonial snack or beverage container, often seen in urban Japanese festivals or storefronts.
description: A cylindrical Japanese sake barrel wrapped in woven straw, featuring a beckoning cat illustration and Japanese text, secured with thick braided rope.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the barrel with dense Japanese text and a knotted rope closure, highlighting the natural straw texture.
description_view_1: This angle reveals the top and side, displaying a white label with a beckoning cat illustration and red-bordered Japanese characters.
description_view_2: The front view prominently features the beckoning cat graphic and bold Japanese text on a white background, with the rope knots visible on top.
description_view_3: This side displays more Japanese text and the rope's intricate knotting, with less visible artwork compared to the front.
attribute_promotional_graphics: A beckoning cat (maneki-neko) illustration is present on the front, serving as a promotional or decorative graphic.
